name: Salesforce
one_liner: Market-leading cloud CRM platform for sales, service, and marketing that exposes customer data and business objects through an extensive family of platform APIs.
primary_auth: OAuth2 authorization code flow via a Connected App / External Client App; server-to-server integrations typically use the OAuth2 JWT bearer or client credentials flows
access_notes: Sign up for a free, no-expiry Developer Edition org at developer.salesforce.com/signup, then create a Connected App or External Client App in the org to obtain an OAuth client ID/secret instantly. For customer production orgs, API access is included in Enterprise/Unlimited/Performance/Developer editions; Professional edition needs a paid API add-on.
api_notes: Very large surface: REST API (CRUD/query on all standard and custom objects), SOAP API, Bulk API 2.0, GraphQL API, Connect REST, Metadata and Tooling APIs, Streaming API (CometD), and Pub/Sub API (gRPC). Versioned three times a year (e.g., Summer '26 = v67.0) with many versions supported concurrently.
mcp_notes: Salesforce ships the official Salesforce DX MCP Server (github.com/salesforcecli/mcp, documented in the Salesforce DX Developer Guide), and Salesforce-hosted MCP servers went GA in April 2026, plus a Data 360 MCP server in developer preview. Numerous community servers also exist.
main_blocker: None; only caveat is per-org OAuth Connected App setup and that Professional edition customer orgs need a paid API add-on.

evidence.claim: REST API authorization is done through External Client Apps or Connected Apps using OAuth 2.0 flows
OAuth 2.0 JWT bearer flow is supported for server-to-server integration without user interaction
REST API Developer Guide documents programmatic access to Salesforce data with seasonal versioning (Summer '26 = API v67.0)
Official quick-start step one is signing up for a free Salesforce Developer Edition org to get API access
Salesforce publishes an official MCP server (Salesforce DX MCP Server) on GitHub

open_questions: Exact pricing of the API add-on for Professional edition orgs was not verified; current deprecation timeline of the legacy CometD Streaming API in favor of the gRPC Pub/Sub API was not confirmed.
